mcq.question: Why is a touchscreen both an input and an output device?
mcq.correct: It displays information and detects touch
wrong: It stores files and prints them
It only sends sound to speakers
It only connects to the internet

mcq.question: Which three touchscreen technologies are in the specification?
How does a resistive touchscreen detect touch?
mcq.correct: Two layers make contact when pressed
wrong: It detects changes in electrical charge
It uses infra-red beams across the screen
It tracks movement using a camera

mcq.question: What is an advantage of a capacitive touchscreen?
mcq.correct: It is more accurate and supports multi-touch
wrong: It detects pressure from a stylus only

mcq.question: Why might a resistive touchscreen be chosen?
mcq.correct: It is cheaper and works with gloves
wrong: It is always brighter than an LED screen

mcq.question: How does an infra-red touchscreen detect touch?
wrong: It detects pressure between two layers
It detects changes in electrical charge
It uses sound waves to detect touch
